a.question: Why transition metal doping of ZnO is interesting?
a.answer:
ZnO doped with transition metals (Co, Fe, or Ni) that have non-compensated electron spins attracts particular
interest as it can induce various magnetic phenomena and behaviors

a.question: Can ALD be used to dope ZnO with transition metals?

c.question: Which material exhibits ferroelectric behaviour?
c.answer: From the results it is observed that Fe-doped ZnO also demonstrates a ferroelectric behavior.

d.question: Do the doped materials show magnetio-optic Kerr effect?
d.answer: The present study provides an extended analysis of the magnetooptical MO Kerr effect and the dielectric properties of (Co, Fe, or Ni)-doped ZnO films prepared by ALD. The structural, magnetooptical, and dielectric properties were considered in relation to the technological details of the ALD process and the corresponding dopant effects. All doped samples show a strong MO Kerr behavior with a substantial magnetization response and very high values of the Kerr polarization angle, especially in the case of ZnO/Fe
